description: Secure Shell — remote access, port forwarding, SOCKS proxying, key-based authentication, and credential brute-forcing.

tools.name: ssh (native client)
tools.description: Standard OpenSSH client for remote login, tunneling, and command execution.

commands.title: Password login
commands.description: Connect via SSH with password authentication (prompted).

commands.title: Key-based login
commands.description: Connect using an SSH private key file.

commands.title: Specific port
commands.description: Connect to SSH on a non-standard port.

commands.title: Execute single command
commands.description: Execute a command remotely without interactive session.

commands.title: Local port forwarding
commands.description: Forward a local port to a remote host through the SSH tunnel.

commands.title: Remote port forwarding
commands.description: Expose a local port on the remote server.

commands.title: Dynamic SOCKS proxy
commands.description: Create a SOCKS5 proxy on local port 1080 (use with proxychains).

commands.title: ProxyJump (bastion hop)
commands.description: Jump through a bastion/jump host to reach the target.

commands.title: Verbose mode (debugging)
commands.description: Connect with maximum verbosity for debugging auth issues.

commands.title: Force password auth (no key)
commands.description: Force password authentication even if keys are available.

commands.title: Ignore host key checking
commands.description: Skip host key verification (useful for CTF/lab environments).

tools.name: sshpass
tools.description: Non-interactive SSH password provider for scripting.

commands.title: Login with password (non-interactive)
commands.description: Connect via SSH providing the password non-interactively.

commands.title: Execute command with password
commands.description: Execute a remote command providing the password non-interactively.

commands.title: SCP with password
commands.description: Copy a file from the remote host using password authentication.

tools.name: ssh2john + John the Ripper
tools.description: Extract and crack SSH private key passphrases.

commands.title: Convert SSH key to John format
commands.description: Extract the hash from a passphrase-protected SSH private key.

commands.title: Crack with John
commands.description: Crack the SSH key passphrase using a wordlist.

commands.title: Crack with John (show results)
commands.description: Display previously cracked SSH key passphrases.

tools.name: hydra — SSH Brute-force
tools.description: Fast network brute-forcer for SSH credentials.

commands.title: Single user with wordlist
commands.description: Brute-force SSH password for a single user.

commands.title: User list with wordlist
commands.description: Brute-force SSH with user and password lists.

commands.title: Single credential check
commands.description: Verify a single SSH credential pair.

commands.title: Specific port with threads
commands.description: Brute-force on a specific port with 4 threads and verbose output.

tools.name: NetExec (nxc) — SSH
tools.description: SSH credential validation and command execution via NetExec.

commands.title: Authenticate with password
commands.description: Validate SSH credentials.

commands.title: Authenticate with key
commands.description: Authenticate using an SSH private key.

commands.title: Execute command
commands.description: Execute a command via SSH.

commands.title: Password spray
commands.description: Spray a password against multiple users via SSH.

tools.name: chisel — Tunneling
tools.description: Fast TCP/UDP tunnel over HTTP. Alternative to SSH tunneling when SSH is not available.

commands.title: Start chisel server (attacker)
commands.description: Start the chisel server on the attacker machine.

commands.title: Client — forward port
commands.description: Forward a remote port to your local machine.

commands.title: Client — reverse SOCKS proxy
commands.description: Create a reverse SOCKS proxy through the target (use with proxychains).

commands.title: Client — reverse port forward
commands.description: Expose a local port on the remote server.
